title: The Lost Toy Treasure Hunt
description: Follow the fairy's clues through the magical park to find your hidden toys! Solve each puzzle to get the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 1: Welcome to the Magical Park! A playful fairy has hidden all your toys around the park. She left you a note at the gate: 'Your first toy is hiding near some flowers. I planted 2 red flowers and 1 yellow flower. How many flowers did I plant in all?'
hints: Count the red flowers first, then add the yellow one
questions.explanation: 2 red flowers + 1 yellow flower = 3 flowers! You run to the flower patch and find your toy car hiding behind the 3 flowers! The fairy giggled and left another clue nearby.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 2: You found the toy car! Taped to it is a new note from the fairy: 'Great job! Your next toy is by the pond. I saw some ducks there — 3 big ducks and 2 little ducks. How many ducks are at the pond?'
hints: Count the big ducks, then add the little ducks
questions.explanation: 3 big ducks + 2 little ducks = 5 ducks! You run to the pond and count 5 ducks. Behind them, you spot your stuffed bunny! Another clue is tied to its ear.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 3: You found the stuffed bunny! The fairy's note says: 'Look at the stepping stones by the fountain. There are 6 stones, but 2 are covered in leaves. How many stones can you still see?'
hints: Start with 6 stones, then take away the hidden ones
questions.explanation: 6 stones - 2 hidden stones = 4 stones you can see! You hop across 4 visible stepping stones and find your toy dinosaur by the fountain! The fairy left sparkly footprints leading to the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 4: You found the toy dinosaur! The fairy's sparkly trail leads to a sandbox. Her note says: 'I buried your toy under a shape with 3 sides. Which shape has 3 sides?'
hints: Think about which shape looks like a mountain peak
Tri means three!
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides! You dig under the triangle-shaped sand mold and find your toy robot! A little flag is sticking out with the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 5: You found the toy robot! The flag reads: 'Your next toy is on a park bench. The fairy painted dots on the benches: 🔴🔵🔴🔵🔴 — What color comes next?'
hints: Look at the pattern — it goes back and forth between two colors
Red, Blue, Red, Blue, Red, ...?
questions.explanation: The pattern is Red, Blue, Red, Blue, Red — so Blue comes next! You find the bench with the blue dot and there is your toy airplane sitting on it! A paper butterfly is attached to the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 6: You found the toy airplane! The paper butterfly says: 'Go to the big oak tree. Count the squirrels! There are 4 squirrels on the tree. Then 2 more squirrels climb up. How many squirrels are on the tree now?'
hints: Start with 4 squirrels and add the new ones
questions.explanation: 4 squirrels + 2 more squirrels = 6 squirrels! You look up at the oak tree and count 6 squirrels. One of them drops your toy ball down to you! Wrapped around the ball is another clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 7: You found the toy ball! The fairy wrote: 'I have 2 groups of butterflies near the garden gate. One group has 3 butterflies and the other group has 3 butterflies. How many butterflies are there altogether?'
hints: Add the two groups together
questions.explanation: 3 butterflies + 3 butterflies = 6 butterflies! You follow the 6 butterflies to the garden gate, and they land right on your toy puzzle box! Inside the box is the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 8: You found the toy puzzle box! Inside it says: 'Which group has MORE? Group A has 5 acorns. Group B has 3 acorns. Which group has more?'
hints: Compare the two numbers — which is bigger?
5 is more than 3
questions.explanation: Group A has more because 5 is greater than 3! You follow the trail of 5 acorns and find your toy truck under a bush. The fairy is getting trickier now — the next clue has a harder puzzle!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 9: You found the toy truck! Now the fairy says: 'You're doing great! I hid your next toy near the swings. There were 7 birds on the fence, but 3 flew away. How many birds are still on the fence?'
hints: Start with 7 and take away the ones that flew away
questions.explanation: 7 birds - 3 birds that flew away = 4 birds left! You go to the fence near the swings, count 4 birds, and find your toy drum hanging from the swing set! The next clue is taped to the drum.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 10: You found the toy drum! The clue says: 'Look at the fairy's stone path: ⭐🌙⭐🌙⭐🌙 — What are the next TWO shapes in the pattern?'
hints: The pattern keeps repeating star then moon
Star, Moon, Star, Moon, Star, Moon, ...?
questions.explanation: The pattern is Star, Moon repeating — so the next two are Star, Moon! You follow the stone path and find your toy bear sitting on a star-shaped stone. A tiny scroll is in its paw!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 11: You found the toy bear! The scroll reads: 'The slide has a special shape — it has 4 equal sides. What shape is it?'
hints: Which shape has exactly 4 sides that are all the same length?
Think of a box shape
questions.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides! You run to the square-shaped platform on the slide and find your toy whistle! The fairy carved the next clue right into the slide.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 12: You found the toy whistle! The slide carving says: 'I left 4 toys on the merry-go-round and 5 toys on the seesaw. How many toys did I leave in all?'
hints: Add the toys from both places together
questions.explanation: 4 toys + 5 toys = 9 toys total! But wait — these are extra toys the fairy brought as gifts! You collect all 9 bonus toys and find a golden clue card among them.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 13: You found the golden clue card! It says: 'The fairy had 8 magic wands. She gave 5 wands to the squirrels to guard your toys. How many wands does she have left?'
hints: She started with 8 and gave some away
questions.explanation: 8 wands - 5 wands = 3 wands left! The fairy waves her 3 remaining wands and a glowing path appears. You follow it and find your toy crown sitting on a rock!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 14: You found the toy crown! You put it on and feel like royalty! The fairy says: 'Count the ladybugs on this leaf. 🐞🐞🐞🐞🐞🐞🐞 How many ladybugs are there?'
hints: Point to each ladybug and count carefully
Count one by one from left to right
questions.explanation: There are 7 ladybugs! The 7th ladybug flies to a tree stump and lands on your toy magnifying glass. You pick it up and use it to read a tiny clue!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 15: You found the toy magnifying glass! The tiny clue says: 'Your toy is near a shape with NO corners and NO straight sides. Which shape is that?'
hints: Think about which shape is perfectly round
It rolls easily because it has no corners
questions.explanation: A circle has no corners and no straight sides! You find the round garden pond and your toy boat is floating right in the middle! A frog hops over with the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 16: You found the toy boat! The frog has a note: 'You had 10 toy coins. You used 4 coins to cross the fairy bridge. How many coins do you have now?'
hints: You started with 10 and spent 4
questions.explanation: 10 coins - 4 coins = 6 coins left! You cross the fairy bridge with your 6 coins jingling in your pocket. On the other side, your toy telescope is waiting!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 17: You found the toy telescope! The fairy's voice echoes: 'Almost there! I put out some fruit for you. There are 3 apples, 2 bananas, and 4 grapes on the blanket. How many pieces of fruit are there altogether?'
hints: Add all three groups together
questions.explanation: 3 + 2 + 4 = 9 pieces of fruit! You eat a snack and find your toy compass under the picnic blanket. It's pointing toward the final hiding spots!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 18: You found the toy compass! It spins and the fairy says: 'Tricky one! I am thinking of a number. It is more than 6 but less than 8. What is my number?'
hints: What number comes between 6 and 8?
It's bigger than 6 and smaller than 8
questions.explanation: The number is 7 — it is more than 6 and less than 8! You take 7 steps forward and find your toy storybook leaning against a mushroom. Only 2 more clues to go!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 19: You found the toy storybook! Inside is a riddle: 'The fairy made a necklace with beads: 🔴🔴🔵🔴🔴🔵🔴🔴 — What bead comes next?'
hints: Look carefully — how many red beads come before each blue bead?
The pattern is: 2 red, 1 blue, 2 red, 1 blue...
questions.explanation: The pattern is Red, Red, Blue — repeating! After 2 reds, a blue bead comes next. The necklace glows and points you to the very last hiding spot in the park!

questions.text: 🗺️ FINAL CLUE 20: This is it — the LAST clue! The fairy appears and says: 'You found 10 toys today! If you put 5 toys in your red bag and the rest in your blue bag, how many go in the blue bag?' Solve this and the fairy will give you a special golden toy!
hints: You have 10 toys total and 5 go in the red bag
questions.explanation: 10 toys - 5 toys in the red bag = 5 toys in the blue bag! YOU DID IT! The fairy claps her hands and gives you a beautiful golden toy star! All your toys are found, and the fairy says you are the BEST treasure hunter in the whole magical park! 🎉

title: The Enchanted Garden Quest
description: The garden gnomes need your help! Solve puzzles to help them find their lost treasures.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 1: Welcome to the Enchanted Garden! Three little gnomes — Pip, Dot, and Fern — have lost their treasures. Pip says: 'A sneaky crow stole our gems and hid them all over the garden! I had 8 gems and Dot had 6. How many gems did we have together?'
hints: Add Pip's gems and Dot's gems
questions.explanation: 8 gems + 6 gems = 14 gems were stolen! Pip looks worried, but you promise to help. Dot points to a trail of glitter leading toward the mushroom patch.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 2: You follow the glitter trail to the mushroom patch! Dot says: 'I see 15 mushrooms here, but only 9 of them are magical. How many are NOT magical?'
hints: Take away the magical ones from the total
questions.explanation: 15 mushrooms - 9 magical mushrooms = 6 regular mushrooms! You look under the 6 regular mushrooms and find 3 gems hidden underneath! Fern cheers and spots another clue carved into a tree.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 3: You found 3 gems! The tree carving says: 'The crow hid gems in groups of 2. If there are 5 groups, how many gems is that?'
hints: Count by 2s five times: 2, 4, 6, 8, ...
questions.explanation: 5 groups of 2 = 10 gems! You find 10 gems tucked into the roots of the tree. That's 13 gems found so far! Pip jumps up and down with joy. A butterfly lands on your shoulder with the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 4: The butterfly leads you to a stone wall! Fern reads the clue: 'The crow's nest is on top of a shape with 4 sides, and 2 sides are long and 2 sides are short. What shape is it?'
hints: Which shape has 4 sides with 2 long and 2 short?
Think of a door shape
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 4 sides — 2 long and 2 short! You find the rectangle-shaped garden gate, and on top of it is a little nest with 2 more gems. Only 1 gem left to find from what you know about!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 5: You've found 15 gems! Pip says: 'Wait — Fern, how many gems did YOU have?' Fern says: 'I had some too! Altogether the three of us had 20 gems. You two had 14. How many were mine?'
hints: Take away what Pip and Dot had from the total
questions.explanation: 20 total gems - 14 gems (Pip + Dot) = 6 gems belonged to Fern! Now you know there are 20 gems to find in total. The quest continues deeper into the enchanted garden!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 6: Dot spots something! 'Look at the stepping stones across the stream! They have a pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, ___. What number comes next?'
hints: The numbers go up by 2 each time
questions.explanation: The pattern counts by 2s — so the next number is 10! You hop to stone number 10 and underneath it, a gem sparkles in the water. The gnomes fish it out — 16 gems found! A frog croaks the next clue.

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 7: The frog croaks: 'Ribbit! I saw the crow hide gems in the garden beds. The first bed has 7 gems, the second has 9 gems. Which bed has MORE gems?'
hints: Compare 7 and 9 — which number is bigger?
9 is greater than 7
questions.explanation: The second bed has more because 9 is greater than 7! You dig in the second bed first and find a gem. Then you check the first bed too — there's another gem there! That's 18 gems found now!

questions.text: 🗺️ CLUE 8: 18 gems found — only 2 more to go! Pip says: 'The crow left muddy footprints. I count 12 footprints going INTO the rose bush, and 12 footprints coming OUT. How many footprints are there in all?'
hints: Add the footprints going in and coming out
questions.explanation: 12 going in + 12 coming out = 24 footprints! You follow them to the rose bush. The gnomes peek inside and find 1 more gem tangled in the branches. Only 1 gem left — but where could it be?

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 9: 19 gems found! Fern finds a locked treasure box in the rose bush. It has a number lock! The clue reads: 'To open me, solve this: There are 25 apples on a tree. 11 fall to the ground. How many are still on the tree?'
hints: Start with 25 and take away the ones that fell
questions.explanation: 25 apples - 11 apples = 14 apples still on the tree! You turn the lock to 14 and CLICK — it opens! But inside there's not a gem — just another clue! The crow is being very tricky!

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 10: The note from the treasure box says: 'Ha ha! I'm a clever crow! Your last gem is in the tallest place in the garden. The sunflowers are these heights: 34 inches, 47 inches, 28 inches, 41 inches. Which is the TALLEST?'
hints: Compare all four numbers — which is the biggest?
47 is the largest number
questions.explanation: 47 inches is the tallest! You run to the tallest sunflower and — wait, no gem here? But there IS another clue! The crow wrote: 'Getting warmer!' Pip says the crow must be watching you!

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 11: The sunflower clue says: 'I flew past the garden clock at half past 2. What does the clock look like at 2:30?' The gnomes need to figure out which garden clock the crow means!

choices: Hour hand on 2, minute hand on 6
Hour hand on 3, minute hand on 6
Hour hand on 2, minute hand on 12
Hour hand on 6, minute hand on 2

hints: Half past means 30 minutes — the minute hand points at 6
The hour hand stays near the 2
questions.explanation: At 2:30, the hour hand is on 2 and the minute hand is on 6! You find the garden sundial set to 2:30 and there's a feather underneath it with another clue written on it.

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 12: The feather clue says: 'I counted my treasures! I had 36 shiny things. 18 were coins and the rest were gems. How many were gems?' Dot says, 'If we find those gems, ours must be among them!'
hints: Take away the coins from the total
questions.explanation: 36 shiny things - 18 coins = 18 gems! The crow has been collecting gems from ALL over the garden. The gnomes realize the crow has a secret stash somewhere. A robin flies down to help!

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 13: The robin chirps: 'I know where the crow hides things! Follow me, but first tell me — if the path is 45 steps long and you've already walked 20 steps, how many steps are left?'
hints: Subtract the steps you already walked from the total
questions.explanation: 45 steps - 20 steps = 25 steps left! You march 25 more steps behind the robin. She leads you to a hedge maze! Pip gulps nervously but you lead the way in.

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 14: Inside the hedge maze! Fern says: 'The maze walls are made of different shapes! I see shapes with exactly 6 sides. What are they called?'
hints: Hex means six
A hexagon has 6 sides — like a honeycomb!
questions.explanation: A hexagon has 6 sides! The hedge is trimmed into hexagon shapes, just like a beehive. Through one of the hexagon openings, you spot something shiny deeper in the maze!

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 15: You squeeze through the hexagon opening! Dot finds a note: 'Left, Right, Left, Right, Left, ___. Which way do you turn next to reach my nest?'
hints: The pattern alternates between Left and Right
Left, Right, Left, Right, Left — what comes after Left?
questions.explanation: The pattern alternates: Left, Right, Left, Right, Left — so next is Right! You turn right and find yourself in a clearing. In the center is a tall oak tree with something shiny at the top!

questions.text: 🗺️ PUZZLE 16: The crow's nest is at the top of the oak tree! The robin says: 'The tree has 14 branches on the left side and 13 branches on the right side. How many branches does the tree have altogether?'
hints: Add both sides together
questions.explanation: 14 branches + 13 branches = 27 branches! You start climbing up 27 branches toward the crow's nest. The gnomes cheer you on from below. You're getting close!

questions.text: 🗺️ ROOM 17: You reach the crow's nest! The crow is sitting there looking guilty. He says: 'Fine, you can have your gems back — IF you solve my hardest puzzle! I have 3 bags with 5 gems each. How many gems is that in all?'
hints: Count by 5s three times: 5, 10, ...
questions.explanation: 3 bags of 5 gems = 15 gems! The crow nods, impressed. 'Not bad! But I have two more puzzles for you!' He pulls out a tiny chalkboard and writes the next problem.

questions.text: 🗺️ ROOM 18: The crow scratches his puzzle on the chalkboard: 'I visited 4 gardens this week. In each garden I found 7 worms. I ate 12 worms so far. How many worms do I have left?' The gnomes whisper: 'You can do it!'
hints: First find the total: 4 groups of 7 worms. Then subtract the 12 he ate.
questions.explanation: 4 gardens x 7 worms = 28 worms total. 28 - 12 eaten = 16 worms left! The crow claps his wings. 'One more puzzle and I'll give everything back!' He looks nervous — he didn't expect you to be this smart!

questions.text: 🗺️ ROOM 19: The crow's final puzzle: 'I collected things all week! On Monday I found 8 things, on Tuesday I found 6 things, and on Wednesday I found 9 things. I gave away 3 things on Thursday. How many things do I have now?'
hints: First add up Monday + Tuesday + Wednesday, then subtract Thursday
questions.explanation: 8 + 6 + 9 = 23 things collected. 23 - 3 given away = 20 things! The crow's beak drops open. 'You solved them ALL!' He hangs his head and pushes a big velvet bag toward you.

questions.text: 🗺️ FINAL PUZZLE 20: The crow gives you the velvet bag! Inside are ALL the missing gems! Pip asks: 'Let's make sure they're all here. We had 20 gems total. We already found 19 gems on our adventure. Plus this bag has 1 last gem. 19 + 1 equals what?'
hints: Add the gems you found to the gem in the bag
questions.explanation: 19 + 1 = 20 gems — they're ALL here! YOU SAVED ALL THE GNOMES' TREASURES! Pip, Dot, and Fern dance around you with joy. The crow apologizes and promises to never steal again. The gnomes give you a special golden key to the Enchanted Garden — you can come back and visit anytime! You are a TRUE treasure-hunting hero! 🎉
